spec_match.rationale: The page now uses :root::selection and a computed-style probe that observes color and background inheritance through the highlight pseudo chain.

interactive.rationale: Theme buttons update the root data-selection-theme attribute, the inherited selection colors, the active button state, and the live probe readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede, code sample, and CSS now all describe the same root ::selection inheritance pattern instead of a universal selector shortcut.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo surfaces the old repeated-selector fallback count, supports an inverted color theme, and reports no-selection versus active selection states.

references.rationale: The page includes the required ChromeStatus link and the CSS Pseudo-Elements Level 4 highlight cascade link.

design_system.rationale: The repaired page uses shared CSS variables, no inline styles or inline event handlers, and Lighthouse reports Accessibility 100, Best Practices 100, and SEO 100.

summary: Re-tested in Chrome 148 through chrome-devtools-mcp at /v134/css-highlight-inheritance/themed-selection/?verify=after-highlight-probe-fix. The previous CSS.supports('selector(::selection)') proxy was replaced with a behavioral getComputedStyle(..., '::selection') probe. Exercised Default, Blue, Emerald, Rose, and Invert theme buttons, programmatically selected a deeply nested block, confirmed the selection indicator changed, and verified the conformance route at /v134/css-highlight-inheritance/conformance/?verify=highlight-inheritance-probe-fix reported 8 PASS / 0 FAIL with no console errors or failed requests.
